title: ACB hospital station unable to induct after robot shutdown state and AGV/tote recovery actions
retrieval_text: Case 223554 at UPS Fort Worth (Haslet) involved the ACB hospital station being unable to induct totes while a robot remained in shutdown state and could not return to normal operation. Source records show the case was opened around 2:11-2:12 AM on 2025-09-26, escalated through CBRE/work order handling, and later documented as resolved after support actions. Teams evidence indicates operators could remove totes but could not add totes at the hospital, with AGVs observed under the rack and at least one AGV showing a disconnected tote association. Recovery actions included removing AGVs 1542188 and 1555028, using API dog to induct a tote to move the system forward, removing tote 51 associated with AGV 1555012 via API dog when hospital removal failed, and re-adding robots. Salesforce closure text states resolution details were to reset Optisweep services and have site restart the Hospital Lane. Root cause is not validated; candidate interpretations include stale AGV/tote association state after AGV removal, robot shutdown state requiring service reset, and recovery complications when tipper stations were not paused.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: Hospital induction was likely blocked by stale AGV/tote association state after an AGV was removed without removing the associated tote.
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Teams follow-up states they likely removed an AGV without removing the associated tote, and later summary references AGV 1555012 with disconnected tote 51 plus inability to add totes at hospital.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: Robot shutdown state at the ACB hospital station likely required Optisweep service reset and hospital lane restart to return to normal operation.
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Salesforce closure email states robot was in shutdown state, issue happened before under prior case, and resolution details were to reset Optisweep services and have site restart Hospital Lane.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: Recovery complexity may have been worsened because tipper stations were not paused while hospital recovery was underway, causing totes to continue routing to hospital.
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Teams summary notes operators need to put the station on pause when not present at the tippers or everything will end up going to hospital during recovery.

they can remove totes but they cannot add

title: AGVs stopped during normal operation with no visible faults after OptiSweep/WCS web application fault and out-of-sync AGV states
retrieval_text: Case 228086 at UPS Haslet, TX is a CAT-1 WCS/Service Failure candidate incident. During normal operation on 2026-02-20, AGVs stopped moving with no visible faults and the system still showed standard operation mode. Teams evidence reported 4-6 bots not moving, inability to remove totes from the hospital, question marks on the HMI, and no failed AGVs. Source category context states the web application faulted at 6:36 AM due to memory usage, causing a service crash and AGVs to lose sync. Troubleshooting included remote access, review of Ignition memory trend and uptime, restart of the OptiSweep/Ignition service, review of AGV status and API responses, and correction of out-of-sync AGVs in RMS. Recovery completed the same day, with the site resuming auto bag-out, but infrastructure follow-up was still needed for memory and log sizing.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: OptiSweep/WCS web application or related service fault likely caused AGVs to lose sync and stop moving.
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Dataset context for case 228086 states the web application faulted at 6:36 AM due to memory usage and the service crash caused AGVs to lose sync; Teams evidence shows AGVs stopped with no visible faults and later troubleshooting focused on service health and restart.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: High memory usage may have contributed to the web application or Ignition service fault, but this was debated during troubleshooting and not conclusively validated in the chat evidence.
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Teams messages asked how memory was looking and requested a 24-hour memory snapshot, while a later message stated there was no indication memory caused a crash. Source category context separately attributes the web application fault to memory usage.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: Service interruption left multiple AGVs in incorrect or out-of-sync states that required RMS correction after restart.
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Source category context for case 228086 explicitly says engineers opened RMS, identified AGVs in incorrect/out-of-sync state, and corrected each one by one. Teams screenshots also show AGV status/API review and mention a zone missing an AGV.

there was no fault or any failed AGVs

title: AGV movement stopped mid-sort with tipper heartbeat failure and hospital tote removal hang
retrieval_text: UPS Haslet CAT-1 WCS/Service Failure incident where AGVs stopped mid-sort, no active alarms were visible on RMS/operator stations, tippers showed heartbeat problems, AVEVA showed tippers closed while field state was open, hospital tote removal hung on processing, remote recovery was delayed by UPS ZScaler VPN outage, and service restart later restored operation. RCA evidence also showed WCSWebApplication database timeout and OPC/OPC-UA communication-related errors, but these remained candidate contributing signals rather than validated root cause.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: WCS/Ignition likely lost stable OPC/OPC-UA communication with tipper-side endpoint(s), causing heartbeat failure and downstream AGV/tipper coordination loss.
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Source context for case 229374 states WCS/Ignition lost stable OPC-UA communication with tipper OTnet endpoint; incident evidence shows missing/zero heartbeat behavior, AVEVA/field state mismatch, repeated OPC communication errors, and recovery after Optisweep service restart.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: Database query timeout errors were present during the incident window but may have been secondary symptoms rather than the initiating failure.
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: RCA screenshots show WCSWebApplication event IDs 10100 and 20102 at 4/4/2026 1:48:37 AM with execution timeout errors; source category context explicitly notes DB timeouts were secondary symptoms.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: OT-side controls or device faults may have contributed to the communication loss.
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: RCA alarm screenshot shows RIO Module Ethernet Failure, ClearLink module not communicating, and VFD 1/2 not detected around 07.04.2026 21:15:49-21:15:52; category context ties these to the likely initiating side but does not validate them as final root cause.

title: AGVs stopped with no visible fault; Optisweep Windows service restart restored production while root cause investigation continued
retrieval_text: Case 229488 at UPS Haslet, TX was a CAT-1 WCS/Service Failure where AGVs were not moving and no fault was reported. Teams and Salesforce evidence show the issue was treated as a recurrence of Saturday case 229374. Early checks reported tippers mostly in run position, heartbeat sitting flat at 550, and no HMI system-level errors. The case was escalated to software support, and the Optisweep Windows service was restarted remotely around 9:27-9:30 AM. The site then started up and returned to production. Later investigation captured OPC UA connection resets involving hospital controller 10.27.80.157, GLD1 10.27.80.248, and OP station 3 10.27.80.156, plus prior-night tipper alarms including RIO Module Ethernet Failure, ClearLink module not communicating, VFD not detected, and IO Link Master not detected. Recovery after restart was observed, but the source does not validate a final root cause for case 229488.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: Optisweep Windows service was stopped or unresponsive at incident time.
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Dataset context for case 229488 states the Optisweep Windows service stopped or became unresponsive, and the service restart was followed by production recovery.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: Underlying OPC UA or network communication instability may have contributed to the service failure pattern.
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Post-incident investigation captured connection resets for hospital controller .157, GLD1 .248, and OP station 3 .156, along with prior-night tipper communication alarms and discussion of network degradation.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: This incident may be a recurrence of the prior Saturday Haslet failure pattern rather than an isolated one-off service stop.
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Teams messages explicitly state the site was having the same issue again as Saturday case 229374 and recommended repeating the prior restart-based recovery.

raw_terms: The IO Link Master is not detected

title: AGV flow stopped at all three tipper lines with heartbeat timeout and hospital tote removal blocked
retrieval_text: Case 229716 at UPS Haslet, TX matches CAT-1 WCS/Service Failure pattern. Sort started normally, then as AGVs lined up to go to tippers all three lines stopped. Nothing was coming to the tippers, all tippers showed heartbeat timeout while status remained active/enabled, and no faults were visible on the RMS screen. Hospital tote removal would not execute until recovery actions were taken. L2 remotely connected, reviewed heartbeat/alarm views, and engineer guidance directed an E-stop of AGVs, Optisweep service restart, E-stop removal, and possible tipper pause/unpause. After restart, AGVs resumed movement and hospital tote removal recovered after a hospital log out/in. Ignition and Windows event logs were saved, and switch log follow-up was requested. Source states same pattern as prior incidents, but exact initiating OT-side cause was not re-isolated, so root cause remains unvalidated.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: Tipper heartbeat failure or loss of stable WCS-to-tipper communication likely caused AGV flow to stop at all three lines and blocked hospital tote actions.
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Source summary for case 229716 states all tippers showed heartbeat timeout with status active and no RMS faults; dataset context notes same pattern as case 229374.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: Optisweep service degradation or unresponsive state likely contributed because recovery followed E-stop and Optisweep service restart.
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Teams and Salesforce resolution notes state the system was E-stopped and the Optisweep service restarted, after which AGV and hospital functions recovered.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: Underlying OT or switch-side issue may have contributed, but was not confirmed in this case.
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Resolution notes requested server event logs, Ignition logs, and UPS switch log follow-up; dataset context says exact OT-side initiating cause was not re-isolated.

title: Startup state stalled with no path to tippers and recurring tipper heartbeat failure pattern
retrieval_text: Case 229777 at UPS Haslet, TX is a CAT-1 WCS/Service Failure candidate incident. The system was put to startup and everything was waiting, with no path shown and nothing reaching the tippers. Salesforce and Teams evidence indicate the engineer dialed into the system, observed that the tipper heartbeat had failed in the same pattern as case 229716, had the client place the master E-stop on, restarted Optisweep, and paused/unpaused the tippers. The system then came back up and running and the client agreed the case could be closed. This is a recurrence pattern, but validated root cause is not established in this case package.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: Recurring tipper heartbeat failure likely caused the startup stall and lack of flow to the tippers.
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Salesforce resolution details state the engineer dialed in and saw that the tipper heartbeat had failed, the same as case 229716, and recovery followed Optisweep restart plus tipper pause/unpause.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: A WCS/Ignition or Optisweep service communication issue may have contributed to the tipper heartbeat failure pattern.
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: The issue is categorized as CAT-1 WCS/Service Failure and recovery occurred after restarting Optisweep, but this case package does not explicitly validate the initiating technical cause.
